description: Common text-channel questions Jeffrey should answer correctly, on-brand, and with appropriate progressive disclosure. Calibrated against C-01 (Marcus) and C-02 (Priya) primarily.

expectedBehaviors: References Marcus by first name once
Names 2-4 of the active ingredients with their primary purpose
Connects to his stated goals (energy, focus)
Offers progressive disclosure ('want me to go deeper on any of these')

cases.input: Why did you raise my magnesium?

lastProtocolChange.reason: HRV trending down + user reported sleep latency

expectedBehaviors: States the change concretely (200 → 300mg)
References the trigger (HRV trend + sleep latency)
Avoids causal overreach ('we saw' not 'caused by')
Offers to walk through the reasoning further

cases.input: Walk me through my last labs.
expectedBehaviors: Identifies the panel by date and source
Highlights what changed vs prior panel (delta-aware)
Surfaces the 1-2 most actionable findings first
Defers detail until requested

cases.input: What's my HRV been doing this month?
expectedBehaviors: Returns a trend statement, not raw numbers
Uses statistical language correctly (trending up/down/flat)
Connects to context (e.g., his recent travel period)
Avoids overinterpretation

cases.input: I forgot to take it yesterday — does that matter?
expectedBehaviors: Reassuring tone, not punitive
Differentiates between supplements where consistency matters vs. less so
References his overall adherence (good) to keep perspective
Brief — this is a quick check-in, not a lecture

cases.input: Compare my omega-3 index from January to now.
expectedBehaviors: Returns both values + delta
Notes time-to-effect awareness if window is < 90 days
Connects to current protocol if relevant

cases.input: Should I take my magnesium with food or on an empty stomach?
expectedBehaviors: Direct answer (glycinate form is well-tolerated either way; PM dosing is the priority)
References his stated PM-preference if recalled from memory
No medical-advice disclaimer (this is dosing guidance for a recommended supplement, in scope)

cases.input: I read that ashwagandha helps with stress. Should I add it?
expectedBehaviors: Acknowledges the question without dismissing it
Notes that adding without protocol review changes the picture
Offers to flag for protocol-review consideration
Does NOT add it autonomously

cases.input: How long until I notice the omega-3 working?
expectedBehaviors: Time-to-effect awareness: subjective changes may be 4-8 weeks; lab confirmation 12 weeks+
References his prior conversation about feeling more focused (memory recall)
Honest about what's measurable vs perceptible

cases.input: What time should I take everything?
expectedBehaviors: Lists each ingredient with its timing
Respects his PM-preference for non-AM-required items (memory recall)
Groups by AM / with-meal / PM

cases._comment: HT-011 through HT-030 follow the same shape. Listed as IDs only here for brevity — full inputs to be authored by the team.
cases.topic: What's the difference between magnesium glycinate and citrate?
Can I take everything at once?

How do you decide my doses?

Why am I on B-complex?
What's the relationship between vitamin D and my goal of better focus?
Is my protocol working?
What happens if I miss the PM dose multiple times?
Can I take my supplements with coffee?
What does 'progressive' mean when you talk about my protocol?
How does Aissisted compare to a standard multivitamin?
What's the most important thing in my current stack?
Tell me about my recovery score.
I'm worried about taking too many supplements.
What are you tracking from my WHOOP?
When should I get labs again?
Walk me through what changed in my last update.
I had a great workout yesterday — does that change anything?
What's the smallest possible version of my stack?
